best_image_source: Arapahoe County Aerials aerial photo dated 03/01/2024
roof_system: White reflective single-ply membrane roof (exact membrane type not fully verifiable from aerial imagery)
roof_age_estimate: Unknown; appears established but age cannot be determined from aerial imagery alone
observations: Large low-slope roof appears generally uniform and intact across both visible building sections.
No obvious large tears, open seams, or significant punctures are visible at this aerial resolution.
Surface shows light soiling and weathering streaks consistent with normal aging.
Minor discoloration and localized staining are visible near edges and some rooftop penetrations.
Several rooftop penetrations/vents and small mechanical curbs are present; flashing condition cannot be fully confirmed from this view but no major displacement is apparent in the image.
summary: The roof presents as a generally sound low-slope commercial membrane roof with uniform coverage and only modest signs of weathering. No clear evidence of widespread deterioration or active ponding is visible in the aerial image, though edge staining and penetration areas merit periodic inspection.
recommendation: Continue routine preventive maintenance and inspect rooftop penetrations, edge flashing, and drains/scuppers during the next service cycle. Consider a close-up field inspection to verify seal integrity and confirm there is no concealed membrane wear not visible from aerial imagery.
